title: Understanding the Problem
text: To find the initial velocity of the shot put, we need to consider the physics of projectile motion. The shot was released at a height of $$2.20$$ $$m$$ with a projection angle of $$45$$ degrees.

title: Using the Projectile Motion Formula
text: The formula for the range of a projectile is given by: \( R $$=$$ \frac{v**2 \sin(2\theta)}{g} \), where \( R \) is the range, \( v \) is the initial velocity, \( $$\theta$$ \) is the angle of projection, and \( g \) is the acceleration due to gravity $$(9.8$$ m/s²).

title: Solving for Initial Velocity
text: Rearrange the formula to solve for \( v \): \( v $$=$$ \sqrt{\frac{R \cdot g}{\sin(2\theta)}} \).

title: Calculate Initial Velocity
text: Substitute the known values into the formula: \( R $$=$$ $$22.22$$ \) $$m$$, \( $$\theta$$ $$=$$ $$45$$ \) degrees, and \( g $$=$$ $$9.8$$ \) m/s². Calculate \( v \).
